title: Klothea Bio Launches mRNA Klotho Trial: First Study With Lifespan Extension as Primary Endpoint
description: Klothea Bio initiated a Phase 1b trial of AKL003, an intravenous alpha-klotho mRNA therapy designed to boost levels of the longevity protein. Unlike most early-stage trials, this study uses surrogate biomarkers for lifespan extension as primary endpoints alongside safety -- a conceptually novel design. Klotho deficiency is causally linked to premature aging in animal models, and boosting it in humans could influence inflammation, cognition, cardiovascular health, and metabolic function simultaneously.

title: Censavudine Trial: HIV Drug Targets Retrotransposons to Slow Whole-Body Aging
description: A $22 million ARPA-H-funded trial led by Brown and U Rochester will test whether Censavudine -- an HIV reverse transcriptase inhibitor -- can suppress retrotransposons, the ancient virus-like DNA sequences that become increasingly active with age and trigger chronic inflammation. Unlike disease-specific interventions, this targets a fundamental mechanism of aging upstream of most known hallmarks. The trial enrolls 200 healthy adults aged 60-65 across three sites and tracks WHO intrinsic capacity across mobility, cognition, vitality, and sensory function.

title: Senosensitizers: New Drug Class Unlocks Senolytic-Resistant Zombie Cells
description: A Cedars-Sinai study published in Aging Cell revealed that existing senolytics clear 30-70% of senescent cells but leave a resistant subpopulation -- which can be reactivated by infections into more dangerous cells. Researchers developed a new class of drugs called senosensitizers that render these resistant cells vulnerable to senolytic treatment. This two-step approach could significantly boost senolytic efficacy and may also address why cancer recurs after senolytic therapy.

title: Nature Medicine RCT: Daily Multivitamin Slows Epigenetic Aging Clocks by Two Months Per Year
description: A randomized, placebo-controlled trial in 958 adults over 60 found that two years of daily multivitamin supplementation slowed the rate of increase on PCPhenoAge and PCGrimAge epigenetic clocks by approximately two months per year. Published in Nature Medicine, this is one of the first large RCTs to demonstrate a measurable effect on validated aging biomarkers from a widely accessible, low-cost intervention. Three of five epigenetic clocks tested showed no significant change, and researchers urge replication.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026 Concludes: Congress Establishes New Paradigm for Aging Research
description: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity concluded April 8-9 in Berlin with a unified message from international researchers: aging is best understood not as a single molecular defect but as a loss of coordination between mitochondria, microbiota, redox biology, and immunity. The congress formally called for moving beyond single-target approaches toward system-level resilience frameworks. This represents a meaningful conceptual maturation in how the field frames longevity research direction and therapeutic priorities.

title: Klothea Bio Initiates Phase 1b mRNA Trial of Alpha-Klotho Therapeutic AKL003
description: Klothea Bio launched a randomized, double-blind Phase 1b study of AKL003 in 21 adults aged 25-75 at GARM Clinic in Prospera, Honduras. The trial uses longevity biomarkers including inflammatory markers, mitochondrial function, cardiovascular measures, and sleep metrics as exploratory endpoints alongside safety.

title: Censavudine ARPA-H Trial: Targeting the Dark Genome to Suppress Inflammatory Aging
description: A $22M ARPA-H-funded study led by Brown and U Rochester will enroll 200 adults aged 60-65 to test whether the HIV drug Censavudine can suppress LINE-1 retrotransposon activity -- a key driver of age-related inflammation -- and preserve mobility, cognition, and vitality over 48 weeks.

title: Senosensitizers Enable More Complete Senescent Cell Clearance in Preclinical Study
description: Cedars-Sinai researchers identified a population of senolytic-resistant senescent cells that survive drug treatment and can be reactivated. A new drug class -- senosensitizers -- primes these cells for removal, suggesting combination therapy could substantially increase the fraction of senescent cells cleared.

title: Multivitamin Slows Two Epigenetic Aging Clocks in 2-Year Nature Medicine RCT
description: Published in Nature Medicine, a 2-year RCT of 958 adults over 60 found that daily multivitamin-multimineral supplementation slowed the PCPhenoAge and PCGrimAge clock trajectories by approximately 2 months per year, though three other epigenetic clocks showed no significant change.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026: Systems Biology Framework for Aging Gains Scientific Consensus
description: The World Congress on Targeting Longevity 2026 convened April 8-9 in Berlin with researchers from mitochondria, microbiota, redox biology, senescence, and systems medicine. Key message: longevity research must move beyond isolated targets to address aging as a system-level coordination failure.

title: PANoptosis in Cardiac Aging: Combining Multiple Cell Death Pathways as Heart Disease Target
source: Frontiers in Cardiovascular Medicine / Fight Aging!
description: New research in Frontiers in Cardiovascular Medicine identifies PANoptosis -- the coordinated activation of pyroptosis, apoptosis, and necroptosis -- as a major driver of cardiac aging, opening new therapeutic avenues targeting multiple cell death pathways simultaneously.

title: High-Dose Influenza Vaccine Reduces Alzheimer Dementia Risk in Older Adults
description: A study published in Neurology finds that high-dose influenza vaccination is associated with significantly reduced risk of Alzheimer's dementia in older adults, adding to growing evidence that infection-driven neuroinflammation is a modifiable risk factor for cognitive aging.

title: Acoramidis Maintains Efficacy in Transthyretin Cardiomyopathy at 5 Years
description: Long-term open-label extension data from the ATTRibute-CM study shows acoramidis maintains durable efficacy in transthyretin amyloid cardiomyopathy -- a common age-related cardiac disease -- at 5 years, providing strong evidence for sustained TTR stabilization therapy.

title: TBX5 Mutation Links Heart Failure and Atrial Fibrillation as Common Disease State
description: Researchers at UChicago Medicine identified TBX5 mutations as a shared genetic driver of both heart failure and atrial fibrillation, demonstrating these conditions are manifestations of a common underlying biology -- a finding with major implications for treating age-related cardiac disease.

title: UT Health San Antonio Rapamycin Dosing Trial: Precision over Speculation
description: NIA-funded trial in 84 older adults sets a new standard for rapamycin evidence generation: determining the minimum dose needed to achieve desired biological effects without excess immunosuppression, directly addressing the gap between off-label use and clinical evidence.

title: ARPA-H PROSPR Awards $144M to Build Phase 3 Aging Trial Infrastructure
description: Seven PROSPR award teams -- four academic, three biotech -- will validate aging biomarkers and create regulatory pathways for drugs including rapamycin, SGLT-2 inhibitors, semaglutide, metformin, and Censavudine as the first human aging interventions to pursue formal approval.

title: Senolytics Pipeline 2026: Science Is Real But Broad Anti-Aging Claims Are Premature
description: Comprehensive 2026 pipeline review concludes senolytics are narrowing toward biomarker-guided, disease-specific therapies. ABT263 and ARV825 are the highest-performing agents across 21 tested; the field's most credible future is localized indications with measurable senescence burden rather than systemic anti-aging use.

title: PEARL Trial: 48-Week Weekly Rapamycin Safe and Improves Lean Mass in Women
description: The PEARL trial -- a 48-week double-blind RCT -- found once-weekly 10mg rapamycin significantly improved lean tissue mass and pain in women, with no increase in serious adverse events. PhenoAge modeling estimated approximately 4 years of biological age reduction in the rapamycin group.

title: SGLT2 Inhibitors Lengthen Telomeres and Reduce Senescence Markers in Humans
description: A 6-month trial of henagliflozin (SGLT2 inhibitor) produced measurable telomere elongation -- the first FDA-approved drug class to demonstrate this effect. The same class also reduces SASP markers, activates AMPK and SIRT1, and modulates multiple hallmarks of aging beyond glucose control.

title: GLP-1 Drugs: Critical Mass of Evidence for Longevity Benefits Beyond Weight Loss
description: A Nature Biotechnology review positions GLP-1 agonists as the first drug class to demonstrate multi-system aging benefits in humans -- reducing all-cause mortality, systemic inflammation, cardiovascular risk, and cancer incidence while improving metabolic flexibility and potentially slowing cellular aging trajectories.

title: Longevity Biotech Q1 2026: $3.74B Raised, Median Deal $21.8M Signals Pipeline Maturity
description: 49 Q1 2026 financing events totaled $3.74B -- up 56% from Q1 2025 -- with deal sizes suggesting companies are graduating from early-stage seed rounds to clinical-stage Series B and C. Full-year projection of $8-9B would represent a 60% year-over-year increase over 2025.

title: Life Biosciences Begins World's First Partial Epigenetic Reprogramming Human Trial
description: Life Biosciences' ER-100 AAV gene therapy using OSK reprogramming factors to reverse vision loss from glaucoma and NAION received FDA IND clearance. Phase 1 dosing began in Q1 2026, marking the world's first partial cellular reprogramming trial in humans.

title: Rubedo Life Sciences RLS-1496 Phase 1 Positive Results for Skin Aging and Dermatology
description: Rubedo Life Sciences reported positive Phase 1 results for senolytic candidate RLS-1496 in psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, and skin aging, validating the approach for dermatological indications and establishing a safety foundation for broader aging trials.

title: Longevity Market Expected to Reach $52.8B by 2035: Strategic Investor Guide
description: A comprehensive framework for longevity investing values the sector at $30.7B in 2026, projecting $52.8B by 2035, with separate investment theses for reprogramming, senolytics, AI drug discovery, and diagnostic platforms. Family offices are identified as leading early-mover investors.

title: Exercise Most Powerful Geroprotective: Dose-Response Mortality Study in PLOS Medicine
description: A major PLOS Medicine meta-analysis quantifies the dose-response relationship between physical activity patterns and all-cause mortality, reinforcing exercise as the most evidence-supported anti-aging intervention -- with stronger mortality reduction than any pharmacological agent currently under investigation.

title: Peter Attia on Metabolic Health as the Common Driver of All Four Horsemen of Chronic Disease
description: Peter Attia articulates that metabolic health -- specifically insulin sensitivity -- uniquely feeds into all four major killers: cardiovascular disease, cancer, neurodegeneration, and metabolic dysfunction. Addressing metabolic health early via exercise, nutrition, sleep, and pharmacology where needed is framed as the highest-leverage longevity intervention.

title: David Sinclair Announces Team and Launch to Counter NIH Longevity Research Cuts
description: Sinclair warns that U.S. longevity research is being defunded at a critical juncture and that he has assembled a team for an April 2026 launch to fill the gap, citing the risk that America loses its lead in the global race to address biological aging.

title: LDL Oxidation and Cerebrovascular Aging: Endothelial Dysfunction Mechanism Identified
description: New research identifies a detailed mechanism by which oxidized LDL drives endothelial dysfunction in cerebrovascular aging, linking cholesterol management to cognitive aging and providing new molecular targets for vascular dementia prevention.

title: Lifespan Heritability Is 55%: Genes Matter More Than Long Assumed
description: Weizmann Institute analysis in Science revised human lifespan heritability to 55% when extrinsic mortality is excluded -- more than double prior estimates of 20-25%. This reinvigorates genetics-based longevity research and suggests current twin studies have systematically underestimated the genetic contribution to lifespan.

title: Longevity Center Europe: DunedinPACE Data Shows Clients Aging 22% Slower Than Population Average
description: Testing over 80 clients with DunedinPACE epigenetic clock, Longevity Center Europe found an average pace of aging of 0.78 -- 22% slower than the general population. At least 10 Polish clients scored below Bryan Johnson's reported 0.65, suggesting structured longevity protocols can achieve measurable biological results.
